methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $110M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Performance Drone Works, founded by Ryan Gury, is a Series B autonomous systems company that has raised $110M+ to date. The valuation universe comprises eight private peers in the Drones & Autonomous sector, including Elroy Air, Zipline, Joby Aviation, Nuro, and Archer Aviation. No public comparables exist in this specific segment.

A peer-median approach was not viable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables; only one same-stage peer existed, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required for statistical reliability. A sector-wide median would introduce unacceptable heterogeneity. Accordingly, valuation relied on stage-progression methodology, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $110M total raised as a proxy baseline, yielding $209M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $178M to $240M, centered on $209M. This single-method triangulation carries material uncertainty given reliance on historical stage multipliers rather than peer-derived metrics. Key sensitivities include the appropriateness of the 1.9x multiplier for this specific subsector and whether total raised materially underestimates current enterprise value. Investors should treat the median estimate as indicative pending access to narrower peer financials or transaction comps in autonomous systems.
